steps.text: Identify the latest publicly-documented GPT-4-class models (e.g., GPT-4.1, GPT-4.1-mini, GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini, o3, etc.) and Claude models (Claude 3.5, Claude 3 Opus/Sonnet/Haiku, etc.), and collect their official context-window sizes from vendor documentation or release notes.
Summarize how context windows are counted and any differences in tokenization or counting between OpenAI GPT-4-class models and Anthropic Claude models.

Research practical performance characteristics and limitations when using very long contexts (e.g., 100k–1M tokens): latency, cost, quality degradation, retrieval accuracy, and memory over long conversations.

Identify recommended best practices from vendors and practitioners for working with long contexts (chunking, retrieval-augmented generation, summarization, windowing) and when not to rely on full-context stuffing.
Synthesize a concise comparison table of models and context sizes, followed by a discussion of trade-offs and practical guidance for choosing context sizes and prompting strategies.
